title: Error: cannot re-exec process
body:
I can't execute any podman command (as a user) after an upgrade of the virtual machine.

I see in logs that these packages have been updated:

* container-selinux.noarch
* containernetworking-plugins.x86_64
* containers-common.noarch

among others.

Any idea?

comments.body: Can you share more information about your environment? From the listed packages I think only selinux could cause problems, do you have any errors in your audit log?

As root, do setenforce 0, and see if it works, if yes then it is SELinux, and most likely you have a labeling issue.  You also could try to reinstall container-selinux package, I have heard about some failures on install.

@rhatdan @Luap99 I am not sure if was related to SELinux but I found out that after the upgrade the user has multiple (additional) entries for `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`. I removed them and now it works aagain. I don't know if podman set up them automatically after the update.

Could you share additional entries which you removed  to make it work.

I am closing this since issue is resolved for user but we still need to investigate about what did you remove to make it work and if it could re-appear.

Before the update:

```
cat /etc/subuid
user1:100000:65536
user2:200000:1001
```
 after the update:
 
```
cat /etc/subuid
user1:100000:65536
user2:200000:1001
user1:200000:1001
``` 
I removed the 3 line to make work again.
So removing user1:200000:1001 made it work.
I add that to my account and I don't see any issue.  Might be a red herring.
